records.specific_details: Fair Deal & Older People in Rural Areas
records.intended_results: Fair Deal Reform::To propose reducing the 5-year land/business transfer clause to 3 years to allow more farmers to benefit from the Fair Deal Scheme.::To propose that land leased under Fair Deal should be classed as “actively farmed” to meet succession criteria.::To propose increasing the ancillary state support loan payback to a minimum of 5 years to allow sufficient time to repay the costs.::To propose increasing cash asset disregard for a single person from €36,000 to €44,000 and for a couple from €72,000 to €88,000::To propose reducing the assessment period for the family home under Fair Deal from 3 years to 2 years, with a maximum contribution of 15% of the home's value (7.5% per year), instead of the current cap of 22.5%.::To propose that income from leased farmland should be excluded or at least partially disregarded in the Fair Deal financial assessment.::Home Support Scheme::To propose immediately introducing a statutory, universal Home Support Scheme, with specific rural adap

records.specific_details: Seeking policy decisions that will reduce the cost of insurance for our members and Irish society as a whole to affordable levels and keep it that way.

Ensuring insurance reform is part of govt policy. Not increasing Personal Injury Guidelines, Action Plan on Insurance Reform

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: Introductory meeting with new TDs to brief them on the vital role pubs play in Irish society and economy, to highlight key issues facing the pub trade and to seek their support on specific policy measures.
records.intended_results: A reduction in excise duty would be the fairest and most effective way to support all pubs and the VFI is proposing a rebate scheme on the excise paid on draught beer/cider.::There has been a 20% drop in licensed taxi drivers over the past 13 years which severely impacts pubs; late-night trade and customer safety. We are asking for support to urgently reform the taxi sector by reforming of entry tests, increasing funding for taxi grants and increase the number of taxis.

records.lobbyist_name: KARE Promoting People with Intellectual Disabilities
records.specific_details: Kare (Promoting  Inclusion for People with Intellectual Disabilities) are members of the umbrella organisation National Federation of Voluntary Service Providers (NFVSP) which represents over 53 organisations nationally. Kare participated in a campaign organised by the NFVSP on the General Election 2026 to highlight issues facing people with intellectual disability and their families.
records.intended_results: To welcome an opportunity to meet representatives to highlight some of the many issues being experienced by people with intellectual disabilities and their families along with the key disability issues directly impacting people living in the different constituency areas.
lobbying_activities: 17 letters were emailed to DPOs across Offaly, Wicklow, North / South Kildare constituencies by email extending an invite to meet to with them to highlight the many issues being experienced by people with intellectual disabilities and their families in their constituencies. Accompanying this letter was a one page outlining the NFVSP key priorities for budget 2026. - Letter

One virtual meeting took place on the 28th July with TD Martin Heydon, Minister for Agriculture, Food and the Marine (Kildare South - Fine Gael) between Kare & Muiríosa to discuss the NFVSP supporting people with intellectual disabilities key priorities for Budget 2026. - Virtual Meeting

One virtual meeting took place with TD Seán Ó Fearghaíl (Kildare South - Fianna Fáil) the 24th July to discuss the NFVSP supporting people with intellectual disabilities key priorities for Budget 2026. - Virtual Meeting

One virtual meeting took place with TD Shónagh Ní Raghallaigh (Kildare South - Sinn Féin) on the 31st July to discuss the NFVSP supporting people with intellectual disabilities key priorities for Budget 2026. - Virtual Meeting

records.specific_details: The Wheel's Pre-Budget Submission 2026 was sent to elected representatives and relevant government departments in advance of the Budget. It highlights the priorities for the community and voluntary sector, including sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.
records.intended_results: To inform elected representatives and relevant departments about the priorities for the community and voluntary sector in relation to Budget 2026.

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Mental Health Bill 2024 and concerns re inability to provide treatment to all who will be involuntarily hospitalised due to current iteration.To provide descriptions using video recordings of people who had benefited from treatment under the current Mental Health Act explaining their illnesses and the necessary treatment provided in a timely manner.
records.intended_results: To provide an understanding (for TDs) of being severely unwell with psychosis and severe depression from people with lived experience and how that manifested for them. To give lived experience insight into how necessary, and importantly helpful to recovery, it was to receive timely and urgent treatment as a patient under the current Mental Health Act and whom were not at immediate risk of harm or harming.

records.specific_details: Blessington Draft Local Area Plan 2025 for Dunmoy's South Blessington Lands.
records.intended_results: Intended to ensure the drivers behind our rezoning proposal is understood, and to aim to ensure that it is conveyed that our submission was well thought-out, researched and fully conveying what we are trying to achieve through our LAP Submission for Blessington. Identifying steps taken to establish facts included in our submission including GPR Surveys, Analysis of WWTP's throughoutIreland, Consultations and engagement with local residents to ensure what we propose is supported by local needs.
lobbying_activities: Meeting with Edward Timmins on 10/01/2025 in Buswells Hotel, Dublin 2 at 2pm. Discussed planning process and how it could change for the better, LAP process, and our specific proposals for Blessington LAP. Brief discussion on our Bray lands. - Meeting
Meeting with Gerry O'Neill on 23/04/2025 re. LAP process, outcome of Councillors Meeting, next steps in LAP process. - Meeting

Meeting with Patsy Glennon, Avril Cronin, Gerry O'Neill, Jason Mulhall 26/02/2025 in Blessington Municipal Offices to discuss submission and proposal for land rezoning as part of Blessington LAP process. - Meeting
Phone calls with Peter Stapleton on 27/02/2025, & 28/02/2025 re. meeting held with other councillors on Blessington LAP re-zoning proposal. - Phone call

records.specific_details: Discussion of the financial strain paced on inter-county players through playing their games
records.intended_results: To inform named individuals of content of an independent Indecon Report, commissioned by the GPA, which examines the economic impact on inter-county Gaelic players.

records.specific_details: We engaged with elected representatives regarding funding for youth and family services in Kildare and West Wicklow. We also discussed facilities and premises across the region.
records.intended_results: improved funding for servicesFunding and support for dedicated youth facilities.

We met and discussed the issues mentioned above. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Bovine TB
records.intended_results: To emphasise that and enhanced wildlife control programme and a better resourced TB eradication programme is urgently needed to reduce the unacceptable levels of the disease.::To seek the support of members of the Oireachtas for an enhanced Wildlife Control Programme.::That the proposals put to the TB Forum meeting by the Department of Agriculture and the manner in which they were presented was shambolic.::That the Department didn't circulate their ‘proposals' to stakeholders at the Forum, instead choosing to flash them up on a screen halfway through the meeting and seemed unsure themselves as to what they actually were proposing. It was a shambolic approach to such a serious issue for farmers, their families and their livelihoods.::That, based on the offering from the Department, they do not seem to have the same objectives and don't appear to appreciate the devastation this disease is causing on farms throughout the country. It was a cocktail of reduced compensation; more and prolong

records.specific_details: Re-launch All Party Group on Rare Diseases. Update on the need for a new National Strategy for Rare Diseases, including background data on rare diseases in Ireland and the challenges that people living with rare diseases face.
records.intended_results: Understanding of the rare disease landscape and the priorities of people living with rare diseases.
